title: Temporary Trade Truce
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
In November 2016, the global economy was already contending with the uncertainty unleashed by the Brexit referendum when another political jolt reshaped the world order: the election of Donald Trump as the 45th President of the United States. His victory, rooted in populist discontent and economic nationalism, marked a turning point for globalisation. The campaign's rhetoric - centred on restoring domestic industry, revising trade agreements, and confronting China's export dominance - signalled a clear departure from decades of open-market orthodoxy.
